name: HymnTrails Modern Echo Database
description: Verified data for the contemporary (and occasionally older) songs used as 'Modern Echo' pairings throughout hymns.json. Exists to replace unverified AI recall with stored, checkable excerpts and connection notes at generation time, the same evidentiary standard applied to the rest of HymnTrails' data.
lyric_policy: Identical to hymns.json: full text only for confirmed public domain (pre-1929) entries; short excerpt (<15 words) plus lyrics_link otherwise.
note_on_connections: The `connection_note` field here is a general description of why a song functions as an echo. The specific, hymn-by-hymn pairing rationale remains in each hymn's own `modern_echo.connection` field in hymns.json — this file supplies verified lyric data to support those pairings, not replace them.

echoes.title: Christ the Lord Is Risen Today
echoes.full_text: Christ the Lord is risen today, Alleluia! Sons of men and angels say, Alleluia!
echoes.connection_note: Wesley's Easter processional hymn; see hymn-026 for full historical notes.

echoes.title: My Hope Is Built (The Solid Rock)
echoes.full_text: My hope is built on nothing less than Jesus' blood and righteousness; I dare not trust the sweetest frame, but wholly lean on Jesus' name.
echoes.connection_note: Mote reportedly wrote the chorus while walking to work, finishing the rest of the hymn after visiting a dying parishioner the same evening. Hillsong's 'Cornerstone' (2012) is a direct, acknowledged update of this hymn.

echoes.title: Crown Him with Many Crowns
echoes.full_text: Crown him with many crowns, the Lamb upon his throne; hark how the heavenly anthem drowns all music but its own.
echoes.connection_note: Combines two separately written hymn texts (Bridges, then expanded by Thring) into a single coronation hymn for Christ, drawing on Revelation's throne-room imagery.

echoes.title: The Church's One Foundation
echoes.full_text: The Church's one foundation is Jesus Christ her Lord; she is His new creation by water and the Word.
echoes.connection_note: Written amid controversy over biblical authority in the Church of England; see hymn-030 for full historical notes.

echoes.title: It Is Well with My Soul
echoes.full_text: When peace like a river attendeth my way, when sorrows like sea billows roll; whatever my lot, thou hast taught me to say, it is well, it is well with my soul.
echoes.connection_note: Written after the loss of Spafford's four daughters at sea; see hymn-012 for full historical notes.

echoes.title: Wade in the Water
echoes.full_text: Wade in the water, wade in the water, children, wade in the water, God's gonna trouble the water.
echoes.connection_note: Carries layered meaning of Red Sea deliverance and coded Underground Railroad instruction; see hymn-015 for full historical notes.

echoes.title: Be Thou My Vision
echoes.full_text: Be thou my vision, O Lord of my heart; naught be all else to me, save that thou art.
echoes.connection_note: Translated from an Old Irish poem possibly dating to the early medieval period; see hymn-014 for full historical notes.

echoes.title: Great Is Thy Faithfulness
echoes.full_text: Great is thy faithfulness, O God my Father, there is no shadow of turning with thee.
echoes.connection_note: A direct paraphrase of Lamentations 3:22-23; see hymn-013 for full historical notes.

echoes.title: Shine
echoes.excerpt: We are the light of the world, let your light so shine
echoes.connection_note: A direct restatement of Matthew 5:14-16's command not to hide one's light.

echoes.title: I Can Only Imagine
echoes.excerpt: Surrounded by your glory, what will my heart feel
echoes.connection_note: Written by Bart Millard after his father's death, imagining unmediated encounter with Christ in glory.

echoes.title: The Heart of Worship
echoes.excerpt: I'm coming back to the heart of worship
echoes.connection_note: Written after a season Redman's church deliberately paused sung worship to rediscover its substance apart from performance.

echoes.title: In Christ Alone
echoes.excerpt: In Christ alone my hope is found
echoes.connection_note: See hymn-019 — this song is itself a full hymn entry in the main database.

echoes.title: Blessed Be Your Name
echoes.excerpt: Blessed be your name when I'm found in the desert place
echoes.connection_note: Draws on Job's worship in the midst of loss, insisting praise is not contingent on circumstance.

echoes.title: How Great Is Our God
echoes.excerpt: The splendor of a King clothed in majesty, let all the earth rejoice
echoes.connection_note: A contemporary companion to 'How Great Thou Art,' moving from creation's vastness to the Creator's greatness.

echoes.title: Labor Room
echoes.excerpt: Labor of pushing, labor of birth, labor of a Father
echoes.connection_note: Insists on the Incarnation's physical, costly reality rather than abstracting Christmas into pure sentiment.

echoes.title: Praise You in This Storm
echoes.excerpt: I was sure by now, God, that you would have reached down
echoes.connection_note: Names disappointment with God's apparent inaction honestly before choosing praise in spite of it.

echoes.title: Same Power
echoes.excerpt: The same power that conquered the grave lives in me
echoes.connection_note: Applies resurrection power directly to present struggle, not only future hope.

echoes.title: 10,000 Reasons (Bless the Lord)
echoes.excerpt: Bless the Lord, O my soul, O my soul, worship his holy name
echoes.connection_note: See hymn-059 — this song is itself a full hymn entry in the main database.

echoes.title: Holy Spirit
echoes.excerpt: Let us become more aware of your presence
echoes.connection_note: A prayer for the Spirit's felt presence to occupy the inner life, not remain at a distance.

echoes.title: Cornerstone
echoes.excerpt: Christ alone, cornerstone, weak made strong in the Savior's love
echoes.connection_note: See hymn-060 — this song is itself a full hymn entry in the main database, built directly on Edward Mote's 1834 hymn.

echoes.title: Man of Sorrows
echoes.excerpt: The sin of man and wrath of God has been on Jesus laid
echoes.connection_note: Refuses to look away from the brutality of the cross before arriving at its meaning, drawing its title from Isaiah 53.

echoes.title: Good to Me
echoes.excerpt: You're my rock, my strength in every season
echoes.connection_note: Echoes Psalm 23's shepherd imagery through personal weakness and need.

echoes.title: Faithful
echoes.excerpt: He has always been faithful to me
echoes.connection_note: Anchors present trust in God's unbroken track record rather than present circumstance.

echoes.title: Jesus Paid It All
echoes.excerpt: Sin had left a crimson stain, he washed it white as snow
echoes.connection_note: A contemporary re-recording centered entirely on substitutionary atonement.

echoes.title: Good Good Father
echoes.excerpt: You're a good good Father, it's who you are
echoes.connection_note: Frames ordinary daily life as an opportunity to encounter God's character freshly.

echoes.title: Send Me
echoes.excerpt: Here I am, send me, here I am, send me
echoes.connection_note: Picks up Isaiah 6:8's call narrative directly, framing willingness to be sent as the response to encountering God's holiness.

echoes.title: Forever (We Sing Hallelujah)
echoes.excerpt: Forever, he is risen, he is risen, and he reigns
echoes.connection_note: Reaches for sustained, repeated praise language to describe a victory too large for ordinary description.

echoes.title: No Longer Slaves
echoes.excerpt: I'm no longer a slave to fear, I am a child of God
echoes.connection_note: Declares fearlessness grounded in adopted identity rather than absence of real threat.

echoes.title: Come As You Are
echoes.excerpt: Come out of sadness from wherever you've been
echoes.connection_note: Removes preconditions for approaching God, extending invitation to the spiritually wandering or weary.

echoes.title: Peace Be Still
echoes.excerpt: From the eye of the storm, you are sovereign over it all
echoes.connection_note: Speaks peace directly into named storms rather than around them, drawing on Jesus calming the sea.

echoes.title: Fix My Eyes
echoes.excerpt: I'm gonna fix my eyes on you, on you
echoes.connection_note: Shares both title and central image with 'Turn Your Eyes upon Jesus,' making redirected attention the whole engine of spiritual change.

echoes.title: Spirit Lead Me
echoes.excerpt: Spirit lead me where my trust is without borders
echoes.connection_note: Frames the Spirit's work as something actively yielded to, echoing 'melt me, mold me' language from older Spirit hymns.

echoes.title: The Lion and the Lamb
echoes.excerpt: Our God is the Lion, the Lion of Judah
echoes.connection_note: Grounds courage in God's character, holding strength and gentleness together from Revelation 5's throne-room vision.

echoes.title: O Praise the Name (Anástasis)
echoes.excerpt: I was once a slave to sin, but now I have been set free
echoes.connection_note: Moves through crucifixion, burial, and resurrection in sequence, landing on worship rather than grief.

echoes.title: What A Beautiful Name
echoes.excerpt: What a beautiful name it is, the name of Jesus Christ my King
echoes.connection_note: See hymn-061 — this song is itself a full hymn entry in the main database.

echoes.title: I Surrender
echoes.excerpt: I surrender, I surrender, every part of me surrenders
echoes.connection_note: Moves through surrender as a deliberate, itemized act rather than a vague feeling.

echoes.title: Way Maker
echoes.excerpt: You are here, moving in our midst, way maker, miracle worker
echoes.connection_note: See hymn-063 — this song is itself a full hymn entry in the main database.

echoes.title: Glorious Day
echoes.excerpt: I needed you, I needed someone to wash away my sin
echoes.connection_note: Treats Christ's victory as too large for ordinary sentence structure, reaching for sustained praise.

echoes.title: Even If
echoes.excerpt: Even if the healing doesn't come, I will still say you are good
echoes.connection_note: Holds friendship with Christ and unresolved hardship together honestly, without forcing premature resolution.

echoes.title: Reckless Love
echoes.excerpt: He chases me down, fights till I'm found, leaves the ninety-nine
echoes.connection_note: Describes grace as disproportionate to merit, drawing on the parable of the lost sheep.

echoes.title: Build My Life
echoes.excerpt: Worthy of every breath we could ever breathe
echoes.connection_note: See hymn-064 — this song is itself a full hymn entry in the main database.

echoes.title: One Love
echoes.excerpt: We've got one love, that's all we need
echoes.connection_note: Makes love the central, visible evidence of authentic Christian community.

echoes.title: You Say
echoes.excerpt: You say I am loved when I can't feel a thing
echoes.connection_note: Counters discouragement with declared identity and worth rather than circumstance-based comfort.

echoes.title: Living Hope
echoes.excerpt: Death has lost its grip on me, you have broken every chain
echoes.connection_note: See hymn-065 — this song is itself a full hymn entry in the main database.

echoes.title: Yet Not I But Through Christ in Me
echoes.excerpt: What gift of grace is Jesus my redeemer
echoes.connection_note: See hymn-066 — this song is itself a full hymn entry in the main database, a sustained meditation on Galatians 2:20.

echoes.title: Goodness of God
echoes.excerpt: I love you Lord, oh your mercy never fails me
echoes.connection_note: See hymn-062 — this song is itself a full hymn entry in the main database.

echoes.title: Yes I Will
echoes.excerpt: I count it all as joy, I count it all as joy
echoes.connection_note: Sustains confident, repeated declaration in the face of ongoing waiting.

echoes.title: Heal Our Land
echoes.excerpt: If my people will humble themselves and pray
echoes.connection_note: Applies 2 Chronicles 7:14's healing promise directly to present communal brokenness.

echoes.title: Raise a Hallelujah
echoes.excerpt: I raise a hallelujah in the presence of my enemies
echoes.connection_note: Uses repeated imperative praise as the entire structural engine of the song.

echoes.title: New Every Morning
echoes.excerpt: Your mercy is new every morning
echoes.connection_note: Draws on Lamentations 3's principle that mercy is renewed daily, not granted once for all of life in advance.

echoes.title: King of Kings
echoes.excerpt: Praise the Father, praise the Son, praise the Spirit, three in one
echoes.connection_note: Traces a king's reign extending to the whole world, filling in the New Testament fulfillment of Old Testament royal Psalms.

echoes.title: Search My Heart
echoes.excerpt: Search my heart, try my mind, see if there's an offense in me
echoes.connection_note: Asks God to do the deep internal work the believer cannot do alone, echoing Psalm 139's closing prayer.

echoes.title: At the Table
echoes.excerpt: There's a place for you at the table
echoes.connection_note: Centers communion as an act of humble gathering before God and one another.

echoes.title: Breathe
echoes.excerpt: Breathe on me, breath of God
echoes.connection_note: Nearly identical request to Edwin Hatch's 1878 hymn 'Breathe on Me, Breath of God,' separated by over a century but asking the same thing.

echoes.title: The War Inside
echoes.excerpt: In the quiet, you meet me there
echoes.connection_note: Frames turning to God as the necessary escape from internal noise rather than an occasional religious obligation.

echoes.title: Holy Forever
echoes.excerpt: The same God that's never failed, won't start failing now
echoes.connection_note: Draws directly from Revelation 4's throne-room vision, structuring the song around unceasing 'holy' worship.

echoes.title: Same God
echoes.excerpt: The same God that's never failed me yet
echoes.connection_note: Insists God's power demonstrated to others in Scripture is equally available now.
